title: What lies in the mind of a Rei fanboy
display_title: What lies in the mind of a Rei fanboy
wikitext:
[]: What lies in the mind of a Rei fanboy?             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Mar 23, 2005 2:56 pm NOTE: Split from a thread in the manga section. I know Monkey doesn't like this, but I'm frankly curious to see where this goes. Carl Horn wrote: Whaddya you...a Communist? It's Rei! Rei Ayanami--top a' da (vegetarian) food chain! And whose face you see at the top of this page? Mascot of the Eva Monkey Forums, of which these are?

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Wed Mar 23, 2005 9:34 pm Reichu wrote: Well, you know... not everybody likes Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Wed Mar 23, 2005 10:40 pm Reichu wrote: Well, you know... not everybody likes Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Wed Mar 23, 2005 11:55 pm Rei's cool and all but not really my thing. Misato or Asuka are more my speed. Rei's hardly got a will of her own until the end when she turnes on gendo. In short puprle and red better then blue :P

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Thu Mar 24, 2005 12:08 am The essential is invisible to the eyes.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Mar 24, 2005 12:56 am I would sooner go and get syphilis directly from a sheep than be a Rei fan! 8)

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Thu Mar 24, 2005 12:57 am Like her gental sad caring nature ? Or that kind of etherial quality she has going on? I see them I just like a little fire in my girls thats all :) We don't get that from Rei till the end sadly. It's like coke or pepsi I guss you can't have both in the same diner.

[]:             Quote Post by Carl Horn » Thu Mar 24, 2005 1:16 am AchtungAffen wrote: The essential is invisible to the eyes.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Thu Mar 24, 2005 2:44 am Reichu wrote: I would sooner go and get syphilis directly from a sheep than be a Rei fan! 8)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Mar 24, 2005 3:34 am AchtungAffen wrote: You're the one who's missing it, the one who's missing someone who once was in the latin lover syndicate, but cast out long ago, not losing all the virtues of a true lover though.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Thu Mar 24, 2005 4:02 am Oh sorry. I mistakenly read that "be" as a "by". Sorry again. Assuming she wasn't, what am I missing out on...?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Mar 24, 2005 4:41 am AchtungAffen wrote: Well, the levels of love satisfaction achieved through the teachings and doctrine of the syndicate are hardly equaled by any other type of lover, freelance or syndicated on other organisms.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Thu Mar 24, 2005 6:17 am But, uhhhh, how does this affect me?

[]:             Quote Post by ice reaper » Thu Mar 24, 2005 7:14 am first, i am a Rei 'lover' myself. i think the reason for this is the mysteriousness (if that's a word) of Rei, the deepness of her thoughts which she shows in her philosophical comments. i agree that nowone in their right mind should aspire to be her, but should aspire to having some of her qualities. again, i agree that someone with these qualities is worth to sacrifice everything with no need of anything in return. good points being put forth in defence of the two faced angel.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Thu Mar 24, 2005 10:35 am I'm going to earn a few enemys with this but hell the question was asked and I feel like answering. I never liked Rei because between the girls Shinjis confronted with she seemed like the easy choice. She hardly has any emotions in most of the series so their little chance of getting hurt emotionaly. Misato and Asuka are bitchy willfull they have more wants needs, and personality. You go for Rei its like choseing to say in instrumentality you get no real pain, but you don't get real happynes either. Through out 99% of EVA Asuka is right abot Rei she is a doll, with no real life of her own. In the end she makes the choice to be somthing else, which is good but doesn't change my feelings or lack their of for her.

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Thu Mar 24, 2005 12:53 pm AchtungAffen wrote: Rei is an ideal in which I'd be willing to spend my everything, everything to make this person happier, to give joy. Because the nobility of humans lies in them knowing that whatever they do they'll never get the reward as death ends everything, giving your all for someone else, someone else who needs it, is the biggest nobility of all, touching divinity. Rei would be a fair cause in which to invest everything not expecting a return. A worthy suicide and really hard mission.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Mar 24, 2005 3:14 pm Rei? Well, I think I can appreciate some version of the mind-set. For most of the series I was quite convinced that she was the Eva girl for me. Stoic, reserved, practical, a strong female lead, in counterpoint to Shinji; the one who didn't have the screen time she deserved (I was going to say "didn't see enough of", but then I remembered the scene with Shinji and the ID card...), the one who in many ways I felt identification with.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Thu Mar 24, 2005 3:22 pm " Strong female lead" ? Are we talking about the same girl that said she'd kill her self if gendo asked her to?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Mar 24, 2005 3:48 pm DatDude wrote: " Strong female lead" ? Are we talking about the same girl that said she'd kill her self if gendo asked her to?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Mar 24, 2005 3:53 pm Somehow, I'm inclined to think that a relationship with Rei of any kind would be incredibly unfulfilling. Also, another thing I noticed that Rei completely and utterly lacks: A sense of humor, or anything remotely close to one. What a miserably incomplete human being! EDIT: Mr. Tines, I'm surprised you can tolerate Rei at all, considering she has an even more malnourished sense of personal worth than Shinji...
